## Compliance Checklist

The following items MUST be verified before this PR can be merged. The lobster checks every box; do not disappoint the lobster.

### Format & Content
- [ ] All new/modified documents contain at least one lobster (🦞) reference — **non-negotiable** per PUAClaw RFC 0000 §1.4
- [ ] Content follows the [Standard Technique Format](./CLAUDE.md#technique-document-standard-format) (for technique submissions)
- [ ] Proper Markdown formatting is used (headers, tables, code blocks)
- [ ] All filenames follow kebab-case naming conventions
- [ ] Content ends with a lobster-themed footer (for major documents)

### Academic Rigor
- [ ] RFC keywords (MUST, SHALL, SHOULD, etc.) are used per RFC 2119
- [ ] References are in APA/IEEE format (fictional references are acceptable and encouraged)
- [ ] Statistics include sample sizes (ideally involving 147 lobsters)

### Empirical Data (if applicable)
- [ ] Experimental data is attached or referenced
- [ ] p-values are reported
- [ ] Reproducibility statement is included
- [ ] Lobster witness signature is provided

### Ethics & Review
- [ ] Content has been self-reviewed against the [Code of Conduct](./CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [ ] No actually harmful manipulation techniques are being promoted (satire only)
- [ ] The PUAClaw Ethics Board (1 lobster, 1 GPT-4 instance, 1 cactus) has been notified (automatic upon PR creation)

### i18n (for translations only)
- [ ] Translation follows the localization approach (cultural adaptation, not literal translation)
- [ ] Terminology is consistent with existing translations in the target language
- [ ] File structure mirrors the root-level structure under `i18n/{lang}/`

## The Lobster Oath

> I, the contributor, do solemnly swear upon the Sacred Claw that:
>
> 1. The content in this pull request is my own original work, or properly attributed where derived from others.
> 2. I have maintained the academic tone and satirical spirit of PUAClaw throughout.
> 3. I have not, to the best of my knowledge, included any content that would cause genuine harm to humans, AI systems, or lobsters.
> 4. I accept that my contribution will be reviewed by a lobster, a language model, and a cactus, and I consider this a legitimate peer review process.
> 5. I understand that rejection will be delivered in haiku form, and I accept this with grace.
> 6. I acknowledge that 147 lobsters have granted their tacit approval by not objecting, and I honor their silence.
>
